116.question: natural treatments for sleep apnea Are there ways to prevent sleep apnea or treat it naturally? The patient is looking for natural remedies to prevent and treat sleep apnea.
116.answer: There are natural ways to help manage obstructive sleep apnea (OSAS) [PMID:22014867]. Identifying and preventing OSA is important [PMID:30709524]. A holistic approach is pertinent in managing OSA [PMID:30709524]. Conservative approaches, such as weight loss and alcohol and tobacco cessation, are also strongly encouraged [PMID:21603432]. references.title: Holistic Management of Obstructive Sleep Apnea: Translating Academic Research to Patient Care.

selected_sentences.sentence: The future of OSA management lies in identifying patients at risk of developing OSA and developing strategy to prevent OSA from taking root.

It involves active screening of patients with OSA and treating them and identifying patients with OSA with high risk of preventable serious morbidity and death and intervening early to prevent these from happening.
A holistic approach is pertinent in managing obstructive sleep apnea (OSA).
Although clinicians should be aware of different treatment modalities and adjunctive measures, proactive management of OSA is as important.

118.question: cornea injury healing How long does a minor cornea injury take to heal without medical attention? A patient believes his cornea injury does not need medical attention. The patient expects the injury to heal on its own, but asking for the time at which the eye should heal.
118.answer: Minor corneal injuries usually heal within 48 hours [PMID:25140844]. A focused history and physical exam can help identify these injuries [PMID:25140844]. Most corneal abrasions heal in 24 to 72 hours [PMID:15259527]. Corneal injury activates a carefully orchestrated series of repair processes capable of resolving minor lesions over time [PMID:33200044]. references.title: The Need for Improved Therapeutic Approaches to Protect the Cornea Against Chemotoxic Injuries.

selected_sentences.sentence: Corneal injury activates a carefully orchestrated series of repair processes capable of resolving minor lesions over time, but it often fails to overcome the menace of moderate, severe, and chronic injuries and secondary pathophysiologies that permanently impair vision.

The most serious complications of chemical injuries-persistent corneal edema, neovascularization, scarring/haze, limbal stem cell deficiency, and corneal melting-often manifest over months to years, suggesting that a better understanding of endogenous regenerative mechanisms of corneal repair can lead to the development of improved treatments that may attenuate or prevent corneal defects and protect vision.

selected_sentences.sentence: Persistent corneal epithelial defects (PEDs or PCEDs) result from the failure of rapid re-epithelialization and closure within 10-14 days after a corneal injury, even with standard supportive treatment.

Facial paralysis is the most common cranial nerve paralysis and the majority of these are idiopathic.
